excluded_defs.name: normalize
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand the depth check logic ($depth > $this->maxNormalizeDepth), confirming that 0 causes immediate abort at depth=0, but not modified

exploration_log.aha_moment: The normalize() method bails when $depth > $this->maxNormalizeDepth, so with maxNormalizeDepth=0, depth=0 is NOT greater than 0, but depth=1 (first recursion) IS. Actually wait: $depth starts at 0, and 0 > 0 is false, so a depth of 0 wouldn't trigger it at the top level. But negative values would skip all bailouts. The fix is simple: just reject values < 1.
exploration_log.hindsight: Straightforward single-method fix. Reading the normalize() bailout check confirmed why 0 is problematic (allows depth 0 but bails immediately at depth 1 for any nested data).

alternatives: Default to 1 if value is too low
key_decisions.reasoning: Task explicitly asks for an InvalidArgumentException. Silently changing the value would hide bugs.

queries.query_text: NormalizerFormatter setMaxNormalizeDepth accepts zero and negative, fix with InvalidArgumentException for values below 1

solve_notes: Added a guard clause in `setMaxNormalizeDepth()` that throws `\InvalidArgumentException` when the value is less than 1. This prevents setting 0 (which causes the normalize() bail-out to trigger at depth=1, breaking all nested data) or negative values (which bypass depth protection entirely).
task_text:
Fix NormalizerFormatter::setMaxNormalizeDepth accepting zero and negative values

`NormalizerFormatter` in `Formatter/NormalizerFormatter.php` allows
setting `$maxNormalizeDepth` to any integer via `setMaxNormalizeDepth()`.
Setting it to `0` causes `normalize()`'s bail-out check
(`$depth > $this->maxNormalizeDepth`) to trigger immediately at
`$depth = 0`, replacing all data — including simple strings and
integers — with `"Over 0 levels deep, aborting normalization"`.
Negative values bypass depth protection entirely, allowing unbounded
recursion. Fix `setMaxNormalizeDepth()` to reject values less than 1
with an `\InvalidArgumentException`.
